payload.title: Ship air-gap signed intelligence bundle + two-machine update-tool workflow (SAGE-equivalent)
payload.description: Per competitor-sonatype.md §0 and §8, Sonatype's single biggest federal/defense win is SAGE: CLI produces a signed daily bundle (vulns, policies, malicious-pkg feed, mirrored artifacts) on an internet-connected machine; a second CLI applies it to the classified side. Fixops must match this exactly: signed tarball, documented schema, verification on import, no HTTPS-proxy handwave. Build a `fixops-offline-bundle` CLI + import-side REST API + UI.

payload.title: New engine proposal: air_gap_bundle_engine
payload.description: Engine proposed by gap GAP-001: Ship air-gap signed intelligence bundle + two-machine update-tool workflow (SAGE-equivalent)

payload.title: Build offline intelligence feed engine with signed NVD/EPSS/KEV/license/malicious-pkg snapshots
payload.description: Per competitor-sonatype.md §6, Sonatype's air-gap value is the curated feed behind the signed bundle (CVEs + EPSS + KEV + license-threat-groups + malicious-package signals). Build the server-side ingest and evaluate path so that Fixops enrichment (Brain Pipeline step 7) transparently uses the offline snapshot when online feed is unreachable.

payload.title: New engine proposal: offline_intelligence_feed_engine
payload.description: Engine proposed by gap GAP-002: Build offline intelligence feed engine with signed NVD/EPSS/KEV/license/malicious-pkg snapshots

payload.title: Publish Fixops on-prem HA reference architecture: Helm charts, StatefulSet, external PG, HA LB, hardening guide
payload.description: Per competitor-sonatype.md §2, Sonatype ships Helm charts + Kubernetes StatefulSet + PG HA guidance + port/hardening list as part of the deployment product. Fixops needs the equivalent packaged reference so regulated buyers can self-install HA without PS engagement.

payload.title: New engine proposal: onprem_ha_reference_engine
payload.description: Engine proposed by gap GAP-003: Publish Fixops on-prem HA reference architecture: Helm charts, StatefulSet, external PG, HA LB, hardening guide

payload.title: Add per-stage policy verdicts (Develop/Build/Stage/Release/Operate) with Warn-vs-Fail per stage
payload.description: Per competitor-sonatype.md §5, a single policy must emit different actions per stage — Warn in Develop, Fail in Release. Fixops's `policy_engine` + `policy_enforcement` + `pr_gate_router` + `ci_cd_gate_router` cover per-context gates but the inventory shows no per-stage verdict matrix model. Extend policy engine so every evaluation returns a `{stage: verdict}` map and every gate enforces the correct entry.

payload.title: Add hierarchical Root-Org -> Org -> App tree with inherited policies and inherited waivers
payload.description: Per competitor-sonatype.md §9, policies and waivers must cascade down a 3-level tree (Root Org -> Org -> App). Current Fixops uses flat `org_id`. Build the hierarchical model with downward inheritance and override semantics. This is a table-stakes for enterprises running 500+ apps.

payload.title: New engine proposal: org_hierarchy_engine
payload.description: Engine proposed by gap GAP-005: Add hierarchical Root-Org -> Org -> App tree with inherited policies and inherited waivers

payload.title: Build auto-waiver engine tied to reachability + upgrade-path analysis with time-bound expiry
payload.description: Per competitor-sonatype.md §8, Sonatype auto-waives violations that are (a) low-threat with no upgrade path, or (b) not reachable. Waivers roll off the moment an upgrade path appears. Extend existing `vuln_exception` + `security_exception_workflow` + `risk_acceptance` with auto-waiver rules that consume reachability and upgrade-path signals.

payload.title: Add upgrade-path resolver: Next-no-violation and Safest-no-change per OSS component
payload.description: Per competitor-sonatype.md §8, developers expect per-component 'Next-no-violation' (smallest version bump that clears policy) and 'Safest-no-change' (smallest patch-level that stays within current major/minor but clears CVEs). This is the remediation lever Sonatype lists first. Build a resolver that consumes the OSS component graph + policy constraints.

payload.title: New engine proposal: upgrade_path_resolver_engine
payload.description: Engine proposed by gap GAP-007: Add upgrade-path resolver: Next-no-violation and Safest-no-change per OSS component

payload.title: Ship Advanced Binary Fingerprint (ABF) to identify renamed/shaded/repackaged components
payload.description: Per competitor-sonatype.md §6, Sonatype's ABF is a cryptographic + structural hash (class-tree / bytecode signature) that matches components even when their coordinates have been changed. Without this, Fixops cannot identify relocated/shaded libraries in fat JARs, shaded Maven deps, or renamed npm packages. Build an ABF engine and match API.

payload.title: New engine proposal: binary_fingerprint_engine
payload.description: Engine proposed by gap GAP-008: Ship Advanced Binary Fingerprint (ABF) to identify renamed/shaded/repackaged components

payload.title: Add function-level reachability via pre-computed call graphs joined with transitive dependencies
payload.description: Per competitor-emerging.md §2, Endor's 97% noise-reduction claim rests on pre-computed static call graphs for OSS packages joined to app call graphs at scan time. Start with Java, then JavaScript/TypeScript, then Python. Extend `security_dependency_mapping` with per-function reachability edges.

payload.title: New engine proposal: function_reachability_engine
payload.description: Engine proposed by gap GAP-010: Add function-level reachability via pre-computed call graphs joined with transitive dependencies

payload.title: Add Material Change detection: risk-surface diff per pull request (new API, new data model, new secret, new CVE dep)
payload.description: Per competitor-aspm.md §4 and competitor-emerging.md §1, Apiiro's hallmark workflow is flagging only PRs that materially change the risk surface. Fixops has `change_management_router` but no diff-of-risk-surface logic. Build the engine that computes: new exposed APIs, new sensitive-data fields, new secrets, new OSS deps with CVEs, new privilege expansions.

payload.title: New engine proposal: material_change_engine
payload.description: Engine proposed by gap GAP-011: Add Material Change detection: risk-surface diff per pull request (new API, new data model, new secret, new CVE dep)

payload.title: Ship Deep Code Analysis (DCA): AST parser extracting APIs, services, data models, PII fields as graph nodes
payload.description: Per competitor-emerging.md §1, Apiiro's Risk Graph resolution comes from DCA. TrustGraph currently has ~1,941 nodes — coarse. Splitting into business-meaningful entities (exposed API, service, sensitive-data field, data-model, contributor) 10x's node count and enables exposure-layer and blast-radius queries.

payload.title: New engine proposal: deep_code_analysis_engine
payload.description: Engine proposed by gap GAP-012: Ship Deep Code Analysis (DCA): AST parser extracting APIs, services, data models, PII fields as graph nodes

payload.title: Add code-to-runtime matcher: map live API traffic back to repo+commit+owner
payload.description: Per competitor-aspm.md §1 and §4, Snyk (Helios) and Apiiro both link runtime signals back to code. Fixops has runtime + code engines but no matcher. Build an ML+heuristic matcher that consumes OpenTelemetry/eBPF traces + DCA entities and writes `runs_as` edges in TrustGraph.

payload.title: New engine proposal: code_to_runtime_matcher_engine
payload.description: Engine proposed by gap GAP-013: Add code-to-runtime matcher: map live API traffic back to repo+commit+owner

payload.title: Ship VS Code + JetBrains IDE extensions surfacing Fixops findings inline with one-click autofix
payload.description: Per competitor-aspm.md §1 §2 and competitor-sonatype.md §7, every ASPM incumbent ships first-class IDE plugins. Fixops has zero. Build VS Code (TS) + JetBrains (Kotlin) extensions that authenticate via User Token (GAP-039), surface open findings in-file, and trigger AutoFix.

payload.title: New engine proposal: ide_extension_engine
payload.description: Engine proposed by gap GAP-014: Ship VS Code + JetBrains IDE extensions surfacing Fixops findings inline with one-click autofix

payload.title: Add Pipeline Bill of Materials (PBOM) engine — signed record of every build step, artifact, and deployment target
payload.description: Per competitor-emerging.md §5, OX Security's PBOM is a cryptographically verifiable record of commit -> build -> artifact -> deploy. Fixops has component SBOMs but no pipeline-step provenance. Add PBOM nodes to TrustGraph so pipeline-lineage reachability ('vulnerable artifact flows to internet-exposed prod target') becomes a first-class query.

payload.title: New engine proposal: pipeline_bom_engine
payload.description: Engine proposed by gap GAP-017: Add Pipeline Bill of Materials (PBOM) engine — signed record of every build step, artifact, and deployment target

payload.title: Add SLSA provenance attestation signer + verifier (screen exists; engine missing)
payload.description: Per competitor-emerging.md §4 and §5, SLSA attestations are now a required artifact for regulated supply chains. Fixops inventory shows `SLSAProvenance.tsx` screens but no engine named for generation/verification. Build signer + verifier + attestation store.

payload.title: New engine proposal: slsa_provenance_engine
payload.description: Engine proposed by gap GAP-018: Add SLSA provenance attestation signer + verifier (screen exists; engine missing)

payload.title: Deliver agentless snapshot-based workload scanning for AWS/Azure/GCP (Wiz/Orca SideScanning parity)
payload.description: Per competitor-cspm.md §1 and §3, customers refuse agents for posture. Without snapshot scanning Fixops loses every CSPM/CNAPP RFP. Build snapshot-based OS/package/vuln/secret/malware discovery via cloud-provider block-storage APIs.

payload.title: New engine proposal: agentless_snapshot_scan_engine
payload.description: Engine proposed by gap GAP-020: Deliver agentless snapshot-based workload scanning for AWS/Azure/GCP (Wiz/Orca SideScanning parity)

payload.title: Implement toxic-combination correlation: produce single Issue per internet+CVE+perm+data chain
payload.description: Per competitor-cspm.md §1–3, the canonical Wiz Issue is 'internet-exposed VM with critical CVE, overly-permissive role, reaches sensitive data.' Fixops has `threat_correlation`, `attack_chain`, `exposure_case_router`, and TrustGraph primitives but no documented toxic-combination traversal rules or unified Issue UI.

payload.title: Add structured query language (RQL-style) over security graph + saved investigations
payload.description: Per competitor-cspm.md §2, Prisma RQL is a power-user feature that lets analysts compose precise queries over cloud + audit + network + IAM data. NL alone isn't enough. Build a SQL-like DSL over TrustGraph with saved investigations.

payload.title: New engine proposal: security_query_language_engine
payload.description: Engine proposed by gap GAP-024: Add structured query language (RQL-style) over security graph + saved investigations

payload.title: Attack-path visualization with choke-point highlighting; 10k+ node graphs render <2s interactively
payload.description: Per competitor-ctem.md §2, XM Cyber's moat is ranking nodes whose remediation kills the most attack paths. Fixops has `attack_path`, `attack_chain`, `attack_surface` engines but no choke-point analyzer. Extend with choke-point scoring and an interactive dashboard.

payload.title: Dollarized risk quantification (FAIR + PGM) with per-BU and per-asset BRS
payload.description: Per competitor-ctem.md §3, Balbix's BRS is the CFO pitch. Fixops has `risk_quantification` + v2 engine; extend to FAIR-aligned PGM with Loss Event Frequency × Loss Magnitude output per asset and per BU. Pair with exec dashboard.

payload.title: Add NL graph assistant with traversal-trace explanation
payload.description: Per competitor-cspm.md §2 and §3 and competitor-ctem.md §1, NL queries are the UX frontier. Fixops `copilot_router` + `graphrag` exist. Extend to emit a traversal trace alongside answers so users can see the graph path the LLM followed.

payload.title: External attack-surface discovery from domain seed with subsidiary attribution
payload.description: Per competitor-ctem.md §4, Falcon Surface seeds from a domain and maps subsidiaries + shadow IT. Fixops has `attack_surface`, `dark_web_monitoring`, `passive_dns`; extend with subsidiary attribution graph.

payload.title: Build Universal Connector for third-party finding ingestion with field-mapping UI
payload.description: Per competitor-aspm.md §3 (Veracode VRM Universal Connector) and competitor-emerging.md §3 (Cycode ConnectorX), a generic ingestion framework for any third-party finding format lets Fixops become the consolidation substrate. Extend `connectors_router` + `security_data_pipeline` with a mapping UI.

payload.title: Publish OpenAPI spec + typed SDKs (Python, TypeScript, Go) and Developer Portal downloads
payload.description: Per competitor-aspm.md 'Absorb' §5, the competitor with the cleanest OpenAPI + typed SDKs wins automation spend. Fixops DeveloperPortal exists; formalize the OpenAPI publish + SDK build pipeline.

payload.title: Graduate webhooks to GA with formal event catalog and retry semantics
payload.description: Per competitor-aspm.md §5, Snyk is still in beta on webhooks; Fixops can win here. Publish a versioned event catalog (finding.*, scan.*, policy.*, pipeline.*, attack-path.*, compliance.*) with at-least-once delivery, exponential backoff retry, dead-letter queue, and signed payloads (HMAC-SHA256).

payload.title: Add User Tokens — per-user disposable scoped machine credentials for CI
payload.description: Per competitor-sonatype.md §4, User Tokens are a federal-RFP checklist item: per-user, disposable, scoped to role. Fixops has API Key + JWT but no per-user disposable token. Ship alongside revocation UI and audit trail.

payload.title: Add FIPS-140 crypto mode + FedRAMP/IL deployment profile
payload.description: Per competitor-sonatype.md §0 and competitor-aspm.md §3, Veracode's regulated-industry presence and Sonatype SAGE target federal/classified. Without a FIPS mode artifact, Fixops fails federal RFPs. Ship a mode flag that forces FIPS-validated crypto providers + signed boot + hardened defaults.

payload.title: New engine proposal: fips_compliance_mode_engine
payload.description: Engine proposed by gap GAP-042: Add FIPS-140 crypto mode + FedRAMP/IL deployment profile

payload.title: Expose explainable scoring: per-finding factor breakdown with weights
payload.description: Per competitor-aspm.md 'Absorb' §2, opacity is a universal complaint. Publish the scoring formula and return per-finding contribution per factor (exploitability, reachability, exposure, business criticality, threat-intel adversary match).

payload.title: Ship AI Teammates console: Change-Impact, Exploitability, Fix-and-Remediation, Graph-Chat agents
payload.description: Per competitor-emerging.md §3, Cycode markets AI Teammates as distinct agents with defined roles. Fixops has `ai_orchestrator`, `copilot`, `ai_powered_soc`, `ai_security_advisor`, `autofix_engine`; expose them as named agents with task queues.

payload.title: Add crown-jewel scoping + business-service tagging for CTEM
payload.description: Per competitor-ctem.md §0 (CTEM Scoping stage) and §1, critical-asset scoping is stage-1 of the CTEM loop. Fixops has `asset_criticality`, `asset_group`, `asset_tagging`, `risk_register`; build a first-class CrownJewelConfigurator + scope manager.

payload.title: Scale TrustGraph to 10k+ nodes / 100k+ edges with incremental updates and <2s interactive queries
payload.description: Per competitor-emerging.md verdict, TrustGraph is currently ~1,941 nodes / 7,324 edges. Apiiro and XM Cyber graphs are orders of magnitude larger. Scale the graph substrate (consider indexing, partitioning, Neo4j or equivalent, incremental recompute) to 10k+ nodes and benchmark.

payload.title: Continuous SBOM monitoring: re-evaluate ingested third-party SBOMs as new CVEs drop
payload.description: Per competitor-sonatype.md §1 (SBOM Manager), third-party SBOMs must be continuously re-evaluated when new intel lands. Fixops has `sbom` / `sbom_export` and SBOM screens; add the scheduler + delta notifications.

payload.title: Launch AI Exposure module: shadow-AI inventory + AI-specific attack paths
payload.description: Per competitor-ctem.md §1, Tenable's 2026 AI Exposure inventories sanctioned + shadow AI (chatbots, agents, SaaS LLMs) and surfaces AI-specific attack paths. Fixops has `ai_governance`, `mcp_router`; add discovery of shadow AI usage + AI attack-path modeling.

payload.title: New engine proposal: ai_exposure_inventory_engine
payload.description: Engine proposed by gap GAP-059: Launch AI Exposure module: shadow-AI inventory + AI-specific attack paths

payload.title: Tiered LLM context router + pre-flight cost-estimate modal (per-rule contextRequirement, user-approved spend)
payload.description: Per /tmp/truecourse-analysis.md §8 (LLM layer) + §9 takeaway 1 and competitor-truecourse.md, TrueCourse attaches a contextRequirement (tier: metadata|targeted|full-file + fileFilter/functionFilter) to every LLM rule and computes a PreFlightEstimate (total tokens, tiered breakdown, dollar estimate) that the user must approve before any tokens spend. Fixops' LLM copilots today spend tokens opaquely. Extend ai_orchestrator_router + ai_governance with (a) a per-rule contextRequirement schema, (b) a context-router that selects metadata-only/targeted/full-file context batches with MAX_CHARS_PER_BATCH=100_000 caps and PROMPT_OVERHEAD_TOKENS=500 / TOKENS_PER_RULE=50 / TOKENS_PER_FILE_PATH=25 budgeting, (c) a pre-flight estimator, and (d) an approve-or-reject modal gating actual spend, surfaced both in the web UI and via CLI prompt.

payload.title: Unified deterministic + LLM rule taxonomy with shared shape across SAST/SCA/IaC/secret/LLM paths
payload.description: Per /tmp/truecourse-analysis.md §3 (Rules Catalog taxonomy) + §9 takeaway 2 and competitor-truecourse.md, every TrueCourse rule — whether fast AST visitor or LLM prompt — shares the same {key, domain, category, severity, description, enabled, type: deterministic|llm} shape. Disable/enable, diff, and UI filtering all work uniformly. Fixops today fragments rule shapes across sast, iac_scanner, secret_scanner, policy_engine, real_opa_engine, and ai_security_advisor. Define a shared RuleDescriptor schema and refactor each path to publish rules against it. Enables unified /api/v1/rules, single Rules catalog UI, and consistent enable/disable + filtering semantics. Prerequisite for GAP-061 (context tiering on LLM rules) and GAP-069 (rule DSL).

payload.title: Violation lifecycle with stable identity (firstSeenAt / previousViolationId / resolvedAt) + LLM lifecycle prompt template
payload.description: Per /tmp/truecourse-analysis.md §4 (ViolationRecord) + §8 (Lifecycle / diff) + §9 takeaway 3 and competitor-truecourse.md, TrueCourse gives every violation a stable identity chain so 'was this fixed?' is answerable without heuristics. LLM analyses are explicitly prompted with previous violations and told to emit resolvedViolationIds / unchangedViolationIds / newViolations — real drift tracking. Fixops today lacks a stable previousViolationId chain; vuln_trend and vulnerability_age rely on duplicate/signature heuristics. Add firstSeenAt, previousViolationId, resolvedAt fields to findings; build a reconciliation step in the scan pipeline that links new findings to prior ones by (ruleKey + targetNode + location-fingerprint); update LLM prompts to receive prior violations and return lifecycle classifications. Prerequisite for GAP-066 (diff-mode UI).

payload.title: Zero-infra file-based store option: .fixops/ per-repo JSON store enabling npx fixops analyze
payload.description: Per /tmp/truecourse-analysis.md §4 (File-based store) + §9 takeaway 4 and competitor-truecourse.md, TrueCourse stores analyses in a per-repo `.truecourse/` directory with LATEST.json materialized view, atomic write-tmp-rename, O_EXCL lock, append-only history.json, committable config.json — enabling `npx truecourse analyze` with zero setup. Fixops today presumes a running PG + Redis server. Add an optional local file-store mode so developers can run `npx fixops analyze` on their laptop without any server, then optionally sync results up to a Fixops tenant later. Files: `.fixops/analyses/<iso>_<uuid>.json`, `.fixops/LATEST.json`, `.fixops/history.json`, `.fixops/diff.json`, `.fixops/config.json`, `.fixops/ui-state.json`, `.fixops/.analyze.lock`, `.fixops/logs/`. Auto-seed .gitignore for logs and lock; config.json is committable.

payload.title: New engine proposal: local_file_store_engine
payload.description: Engine proposed by gap GAP-064: Zero-infra file-based store option: .fixops/ per-repo JSON store enabling npx fixops analyze

payload.title: Architecture-aware graph: services->layers->modules->methods + DB connections + traced flows absorbed into TrustGraph
payload.description: Per /tmp/truecourse-analysis.md §4 (Graph) + §9 takeaway 5 and competitor-truecourse.md, TrueCourse materializes services -> layers (data|api|service|external with 0-100 confidence + evidence[]) -> modules (class|interface|standalone) -> methods, plus databases (tables, FK relations, driver, connectedServices) and flows (trigger + entryMethod + steps[] with stepType call|http|db-read|db-write|event). Fixops' TrustGraph has the bus + indexer but lacks the layer classifier, flow tracer, and DB-connection overlay. Absorbing these lets Fixops correlate a SAST finding with architectural context ('SQLi in API-layer module of a public-facing service' vs 'SQLi in file X'). Prerequisite for GAP-066 diff-mode UI.

payload.title: Diff-mode graph UI: dim unaffected nodes, badge affected with new/resolved counts, stale-baseline banners
payload.description: Per /tmp/truecourse-analysis.md §1 (Graphs tab) + §8 (DiffSnapshot.affectedNodeIds) + §9 takeaway 10 and competitor-truecourse.md, TrueCourse renders a 'what changed in this PR' view by dimming unaffected nodes in the React Flow graph, badging affected nodes with new/resolved finding counts, and surfacing 'stale baseline' banners when the current analysis is older than the working tree. Fixops PR gate surfaces findings but not architectural-graph diff. Pairs with GAP-063 (stable violation identity) and GAP-011 (material change). Implement DiffSnapshot computation + UI overlay on TrustGraph.

payload.title: Claude Code Skills as first-class UX: /fixops-scan, /fixops-triage, /fixops-suppress, /fixops-hooks
payload.description: Per /tmp/truecourse-analysis.md §7 (Integrations) + §9 takeaway 9 and competitor-truecourse.md, TrueCourse ships four Claude Code skills (truecourse-analyze, -list, -fix, -hooks) under tools/cli/skills/truecourse/<name>/SKILL.md with YAML frontmatter (user_invocable: true + triggers[]) and numbered Markdown playbooks that call `npx -y truecourse …`. Installed into <repo>/.claude/skills/truecourse/ on first analyze with an opt-in prompt. Fixops should ship /fixops-scan, /fixops-triage, /fixops-suppress, /fixops-hooks with the same pattern so Claude-Code-first developer workflows include Fixops by default.

payload.title: New engine proposal: claude_skills_packaging_engine
payload.description: Engine proposed by gap GAP-067: Claude Code Skills as first-class UX: /fixops-scan, /fixops-triage, /fixops-suppress, /fixops-hooks

payload.title: Committed YAML hook policy (.fixops/hooks.yaml) with no-hidden-defaults guardrail
payload.description: Per /tmp/truecourse-analysis.md §7 (Git integration) + §9 takeaway 8 and competitor-truecourse.md, TrueCourse's pre-commit hook is 100% driven by .truecourse/hooks.yaml with an explicit no-hidden-defaults guardrail (`pre-commit: { block-on: [critical, high], llm: false }`). Fixops pr_gate_router gates at PR time but has no local committable YAML hook policy for pre-commit. Add `fixops hooks install/uninstall/status/run` CLI verbs + a committed .fixops/hooks.yaml read at hook invocation. No implicit server-side state — the YAML file is the source of truth.

payload.title: Leapfrog: YAML/JSON rule DSL for dynamic rule authoring (no fork + rebuild) + VS Code extension pairing
payload.description: Per /tmp/truecourse-analysis.md §6 (Extensibility) + §9 takeaway 12 ('Two structural gaps to beat') and competitor-truecourse.md, TrueCourse explicitly lacks (a) a dynamic rule authoring system (custom rules require fork + rebuild) and (b) a VS Code extension. Fixops can leapfrog on both. This task delivers (1) a YAML/JSON rule DSL (aligned with GAP-062 shared taxonomy) that lets customers drop in a rule file and have it loaded into the sast/iac_scanner/secret_scanner/policy_engine runtime without a rebuild, and (2) coordinates with GAP-014 (IDE extension) for the VS Code side. DSL supports deterministic rules (via OPA/Rego for policy rules, tree-sitter matcher for AST rules) and LLM rules (with prompt + contextRequirement from GAP-061).

payload.title: New engine proposal: dynamic_rule_dsl_engine
payload.description: Engine proposed by gap GAP-069: Leapfrog: YAML/JSON rule DSL for dynamic rule authoring (no fork + rebuild) + VS Code extension pairing
